curriculum.subject: Science
curriculum.overallDescription: 6 units, 6 chapters, 1 topics, 0 subtopics, and 27 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.
coreObjectives: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).
Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions).
Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible).

Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes.

Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction.
Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA).
Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five kingdoms.
Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms).
Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level.
Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations).

Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms.

Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension.

units.description: Unit 1: Matter and its Interactions, Unit 2: The Physical World, Unit 3: The Living World, Life Processes and Heredity (Implicit Unit based on CG 3), Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4), Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas (Implicit Unit based on CG 5), Contribution of India to Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 6), Current Discoveries and Frontiers in Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 7), Nature of Science by Doing Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 8), Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour, World of Living, Natural Phenomena, Unit IV: Effects of Current, Unit V: Natural Resources, Unit-II (Additional), Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water
topics: Unit 1: Matter and its Interactions
Unit 2: The Physical World
Unit 3: The Living World

Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water

Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution.
Studying the properties of acids and bases (HCl & NaOH) on the basis of their reaction with: Litmus solution (Blue/Red), Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate.

Performing and observing the following reactions and classifying them into: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Double displacement reaction. Action of water on quicklime, Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, Iron nails kept in copper sulphate solution, Reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions.

Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on the following salt solutions: ZnSO4 (aq), FeSO4 (aq), CuSO4 (aq), Al2(SO4)3 (aq). Arranging Zn, Fe, Cu and Al (metals) in the decreasing order of reactivity based on the above result.

Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction
Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table and the Modern, Metallic and Non-metallic Properties

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction

Reflection

Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life.
Electric power, Interrelation between P, V, I and R.

Magnetic effects of current: Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current.
Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC. Domestic electric circuits.

units.unit_name: Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)

C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level

subtopics: Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations
Periodic Classification of Elements
Acids, Bases and Salts
Metals and Non-metals
Carbon and its Compounds
subtopics: Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction
Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table and the Modern, Metallic and Non-metallic Properties

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

formative_content_refs.title: Practicals: Finding pH, Properties of Acids and Bases, Chemical Reactions, Reactivity Series
Practicals: Dependence of Potential Difference on Current

practicals.title: Finding pH of Various Samples
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Properties of Acids and Bases
practicals.details: Study the properties of acids (HCl) and bases (NaOH) based on their reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal, and Solid sodium carbonate.

practicals.title: Classification of Chemical Reactions
practicals.details: Perform and observe reactions including action of water on quicklime, action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, iron nails in copper sulphate solution, and reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions. Classify them as combination, decomposition, displacement, or double displacement.

practicals.title: Reactivity Series of Metals
practicals.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al metals on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3). Arrange the metals in decreasing order of reactivity based on results.

practicals.title: Ohm's Law Verification
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent Resistance Calculation
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata Observation
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration and CO2 Release
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of Acetic Acid
practicals.details: Study the properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid) including odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate.

practicals.title: Soap Cleaning Capacity Test
practicals.details: Study the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal Length Determination
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Refraction through Glass Slab
practicals.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, and angle of emergence.

practicals.title: Binary Fission and Budding Study
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Light Refraction through Prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo Identification
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Periodic Classification of Elements
activities.details: Learners read about Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, and the Modern Periodic Table. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to reinforce understanding without summative assessment stress.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Heredity and Evolution
activities.details: Learners read about Acquired and Inherited Traits, Speciation, Evolution and Classification, Tracing Evolutionary Relationships, Fossils, and Human Evolution. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Electric Effects of Current
activities.details: Learners read about Motors, Electromagnetic Induction, and Electric Generators. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to gain conceptual clarity.

activities.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
activities.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

activities.title: Science in Arts and Literature Exploration
activities.details: Explore how literature and arts have influenced Science.

projects.title: Construct Simple Machines
projects.details: Demonstrate the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines, specifically a system of levers and pulleys.

projects.title: Environmental Problem Solutions Project
projects.details: Investigate environmental problems such as ozone depletion and waste production, and propose solutions for biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour (25 Marks)
Unit II: World of Living (25 Marks)
Unit IV: Effects of Current (13 Marks)

Application of Knowledge/Concepts (Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish)
Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments (14 items covering Units I, II, III, IV)
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science - Class X (CBSE Publication)
Topics assessed formatively only: Periodic Classification of Elements, Heredity and Evolution, Electric Effects of Electric Current (Portfolio/Periodic Assessment credit)
project_assessment: Portfolio preparation for non-examined topics (Periodic Classification, Heredity & Evolution, Electric Effects)
Joyful and experiential opportunities for internal assessment

units.unit_name: Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)
topics: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level
C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection
subtopics: Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)

Use of muscles in dance form and sports

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations
Periodic Classification of Elements
Acids, Bases and Salts
Metals and Non-metals
Carbon and its Compounds
subtopics: Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction
Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table and the Modern, Metallic and Non-metallic Properties

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations

formative_content_refs.title: Practicals: Finding pH, Properties of Acids and Bases, Chemical Reactions, Reactivity Series
Practicals: Dependence of Potential Difference on Current

practicals.title: Finding pH of Various Samples
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Properties of Acids and Bases
practicals.details: Study the properties of acids (HCl) and bases (NaOH) based on their reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal, and Solid sodium carbonate.

practicals.title: Classification of Chemical Reactions
practicals.details: Perform and observe reactions including action of water on quicklime, action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, iron nails in copper sulphate solution, and reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions. Classify them as combination, decomposition, displacement, or double displacement.

practicals.title: Reactivity Series of Metals
practicals.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al metals on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3). Arrange the metals in decreasing order of reactivity based on results.

practicals.title: Ohm's Law Verification
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent Resistance Calculation
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata Observation
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration and CO2 Release
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of Acetic Acid
practicals.details: Study the properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid) including odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate.

practicals.title: Soap Cleaning Capacity Test
practicals.details: Study the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal Length Determination
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Refraction through Glass Slab
practicals.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, and angle of emergence.

practicals.title: Binary Fission and Budding Study
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Light Refraction through Prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo Identification
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Periodic Classification of Elements
activities.details: Learners read about Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, and the Modern Periodic Table. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to reinforce understanding without summative assessment stress.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Heredity and Evolution
activities.details: Learners read about Acquired and Inherited Traits, Speciation, Evolution and Classification, Tracing Evolutionary Relationships, Fossils, and Human Evolution. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Electric Effects of Current
activities.details: Learners read about Motors, Electromagnetic Induction, and Electric Generators. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to gain conceptual clarity.

activities.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
activities.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

activities.title: Science in Arts and Literature Exploration
activities.details: Explore how literature and arts have influenced Science.

projects.title: Construct Simple Machines
projects.details: Demonstrate the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines, specifically a system of levers and pulleys.

projects.title: Environmental Problem Solutions Project
projects.details: Investigate environmental problems such as ozone depletion and waste production, and propose solutions for biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour (25 Marks)
Unit II: World of Living (25 Marks)
Unit IV: Effects of Current (13 Marks)
question_paper_design: Typology of Questions: VSA (including objective type), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions
bloom_levels: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize)
Application of Knowledge/Concepts (Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish)
Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments (14 items covering Units I, II, III, IV)
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science - Class X (CBSE Publication)
Topics assessed formatively only: Periodic Classification of Elements, Heredity and Evolution, Electric Effects of Electric Current (Portfolio/Periodic Assessment credit)
project_assessment: Portfolio preparation for non-examined topics (Periodic Classification, Heredity & Evolution, Electric Effects)
Joyful and experiential opportunities for internal assessment

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations

formative_content_refs.title: Practicals: Finding pH, Properties of Acids and Bases, Chemical Reactions, Reactivity Series
Practicals: Dependence of Potential Difference on Current

topics: Classification of elements in the Periodic Table
Formation of compounds based on atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency)
Nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, mixtures, solutions, colloids, suspensions)
Chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid-base, metal-non-metal, reversible-irreversible)
key_concepts: CG 1: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level
C 1.1: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table
C 1.2: Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances
C 1.3: Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes

topics: Newton's laws and effect of forces (displacement, direction, velocity, acceleration, uniform circular motion, gravity)
Graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
Relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation
Laws of motion connection
Image characteristics with lenses (focus, centre of curvature) and ray diagrams
Combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)
Circuit characteristics (current, voltage, resistance) and Ohm's law
Everyday usage of electricity (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
Work in scientific terms and energy relationships (potential and kinetic energy, conservation of energy)
Mechanical advantage and simple machines (levers and pulleys)
Origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude)
Sound propagation through different instruments
Interconnected systems affecting life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere)
key_concepts: CG 2: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
C 2.1: Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces
C 2.2: Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation
C 2.3: Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses
C 2.4: Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit
C 2.5: Defines work in scientific terms
C 2.6: Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage
C 2.7: Describes the origin and properties of sound
C 2.8: Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth

topics: Structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
Role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall)
Semi-permeability of cell membrane
key_concepts: CG 3: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
C 3.1: Explains the role of cellular components

units.unit_name: Life Processes and Heredity (Implicit Unit based on CG 3)
topics: C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition, transport, exchange of materials, and reproduction
C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation
subtopics: Photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals
Transport of water in plants; circulation in animals
Changes in the sequence of DNA

units.unit_name: Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)
topics: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level
C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection
subtopics: Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)

units.unit_name: Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas (Implicit Unit based on CG 5)
topics: C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science
C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics
C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects
subtopics: Literature and arts influence on Science
Case studies: Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity
Sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music
Use of muscles in dance form and sports

units.unit_name: Contribution of India to Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 6)
topics: C 6.1 – Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

units.unit_name: Current Discoveries and Frontiers in Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 7)
topics: C 7.1 – States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied
C 7.2 – States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents

topics: Life processes
Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
Reproduction
Heredity
subtopics: 'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones

Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction

Refraction

topics: Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections, applications of spherical mirrors and lenses.
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light, applications in daily life (excluding colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset).
Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life.
Electric power, Interrelation between P, V, I and R.

Magnetic effects of current: Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current.

key_concepts: The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.

topics: Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.
Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution.
Studying the properties of acids and bases (HCl & NaOH) on the basis of their reaction with: Litmus solution (Blue/Red), Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate.

Performing and observing the following reactions and classifying them into: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Double displacement reaction. Action of water on quicklime, Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, Iron nails kept in copper sulphate solution, Reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions.

Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on the following salt solutions: ZnSO4 (aq), FeSO4 (aq), CuSO4 (aq), Al2(SO4)3 (aq). Arranging Zn, Fe, Cu and Al (metals) in the decreasing order of reactivity based on the above result.
Studying the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it and determine its resistance. Also plotting a graph between V and I.
Study of the following properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid): Odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate
Preparing a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata
Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration
Studying binary fission in Amoeba, and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides
Determination of the focal length of Concave mirror and Convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object

Tracing the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, angle of emergence and interpret the result
Tracing the path of the rays of light through a glass prism
Determination of the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel
Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram or red kidney bean)

units.unit_name: Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4)
topics: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level
C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection
subtopics: Biological organisation (organisms to ecosystems and biomes)

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations

formative_content_refs.title: Practicals: Finding pH, Properties of Acids and Bases, Chemical Reactions, Reactivity Series
Practicals: Dependence of Potential Difference on Current

practicals.title: Finding pH of Various Samples
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Properties of Acids and Bases
practicals.details: Study the properties of acids (HCl) and bases (NaOH) based on their reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal, and Solid sodium carbonate.

practicals.title: Classification of Chemical Reactions
practicals.details: Perform and observe reactions including action of water on quicklime, action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, iron nails in copper sulphate solution, and reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions. Classify them as combination, decomposition, displacement, or double displacement.

practicals.title: Reactivity Series of Metals
practicals.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al metals on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3). Arrange the metals in decreasing order of reactivity based on results.

practicals.title: Ohm's Law Verification
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent Resistance Calculation
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata Observation
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration and CO2 Release
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of Acetic Acid
practicals.details: Study the properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid) including odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate.

practicals.title: Soap Cleaning Capacity Test
practicals.details: Study the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal Length Determination
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Refraction through Glass Slab
practicals.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, and angle of emergence.

practicals.title: Binary Fission and Budding Study
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Light Refraction through Prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo Identification
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Periodic Classification of Elements
activities.details: Learners read about Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, and the Modern Periodic Table. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to reinforce understanding without summative assessment stress.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Heredity and Evolution
activities.details: Learners read about Acquired and Inherited Traits, Speciation, Evolution and Classification, Tracing Evolutionary Relationships, Fossils, and Human Evolution. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Electric Effects of Current
activities.details: Learners read about Motors, Electromagnetic Induction, and Electric Generators. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to gain conceptual clarity.

activities.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
activities.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

activities.title: Science in Arts and Literature Exploration
activities.details: Explore how literature and arts have influenced Science.

projects.title: Construct Simple Machines
projects.details: Demonstrate the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines, specifically a system of levers and pulleys.

projects.title: Environmental Problem Solutions Project
projects.details: Investigate environmental problems such as ozone depletion and waste production, and propose solutions for biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

Joyful and experiential opportunities for internal assessment

competency_groups.title: Explores the world of matter, its interactions, and properties at the atomic level
competency_groups.description: Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, explains compound formation based on atomic structure and properties, investigates nature and properties of chemical substances, and represents chemical interactions using symbols and equations.
competencies: C 1.1 – Describes classification of elements in the Periodic Table, and explains how compounds (including carbon compounds) are formed based on the atomic structure (Bohr's model) and properties (valency).

C 1.2 – Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions)
C 1.3 – Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible)

competency_groups.title: Explores the physical world around them, and understands scientific principles and laws based on observations and analysis
competency_groups.description: Applies Newton's laws to explain forces, explains mass-weight relationships via universal gravitation, manipulates lens properties to observe image characteristics, analyses circuit characteristics and Ohm's law, represents energy relationships mathematically, demonstrates mechanical advantage, describes sound properties, and explores interconnected Earth systems.
competencies: C 2.1 – Applies Newton's laws to explain the effect of forces (change in state of motion — displacement and direction, velocity and acceleration, uniform circular motion, acceleration due to gravity) and analyses graphical and mathematical representations of motion in one dimension
C 2.2 – Explains the relationship between mass and weight using universal law of gravitation, and connect it to the laws of motion

C 2.3 – Manipulates the position of object and properties of lenses (focus, centre of curvature) to observe image characteristics and correspondence with a ray diagram, and extends this understanding to a combination of lenses (telescope, microscope)

C 2.4 – Manipulates and analyses different characteristics of the circuit (current, voltage, resistance) and mathematises their relationship (Ohm's law), and applies it to everyday usage (electricity bill, short circuit, safety measures)
C 2.5 – Defines work in scientific terms, and represents the relationship between potential and kinetic energy (conservation of energy) in mathematical expressions
C 2.6 – Demonstrates the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines (system of levers and pulleys)
C 2.7 – Describes the origin and properties of sound (wavelength, frequency, amplitude) and differences in what we hear as it propagates through different instruments

C 2.8 – Explores interconnected systems and phenomena that support and affect life on Earth (hydrosphere, biosphere, atmosphere, geosphere, cryosphere and their interrelationships, earth processes, hazards, etc.)

competency_groups.title: Explores the structure and function of the living world at the cellular level
competency_groups.description: Explains the role of cellular components in making cells the structural basis of life, analyses similarities and differences in life processes (nutrition, transport, exchange, reproduction), and describes mechanisms of heredity.
competencies: C 3.1 – Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes

C 3.2 – Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction
C 3.3 – Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA)

competency_groups.title: Explores interconnectedness between organisms and their environment
competency_groups.description: Applies knowledge of cellular diversity and ecological roles to classify organisms, illustrates levels of biological organization, analyses interactions at organism to ecosystem levels, analyses patterns of inheritance and population consequences, and analyses evidence of biological evolution.
competencies: C 4.1 – Applies the knowledge of cellular diversity in organisms along with the ecological role organisms play (autotrophic or heterotrophic nutrition) to classify them into five kingdoms
C 4.2 – Illustrates different levels of organisations of living organisms (from molecules to organisms)
C 4.3 – Analyses different levels of biological organisation from organisms to ecosystems and biomes along with interactions that take place at each level
C 4.4 – Analyses patterns of inheritance of traits in terms of Mendel's laws and its consequences at a population level (using models and/or simulations)

C 4.5 – Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms

competency_groups.title: Draws linkages between scientific knowledge and knowledge across other curricular areas
competency_groups.description: Explores influence of literature and arts on Science, examines case studies related to Science in human life from Social Sciences and Ethics perspectives, and applies scientific principles to phenomena in other subjects.
competencies: C 5.1 – Explores how literature and arts have influenced Science

C 5.2 – Examines a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (for example, Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity)
C 5.3 – Applies scientific principles to explain phenomena in other subjects (sound pitch, octave, and amplitude in music; use of muscles in dance form and sports)

competency_groups.title: Understands and appreciates the contribution of India through history, and the present time to the overall field of Science
competency_groups.description: Knows and explains significant contributions of India to all matters studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner.
competencies: C 6.1 – Knows and explains the significant contributions of India to all matters (concepts, explanations, methods) that are studied within the curriculum in an integrated manner

competency_groups.title: Develops awareness of the most current discoveries, ideas, and frontiers in all areas of scientific knowledge
competency_groups.description: States concepts representing current understanding of matter and states questions related to matters where current scientific understanding is well-recognised.
competencies: C 7.1 – States concepts that represent the most current understanding of the matter being studied, ranging from mere familiarity to conceptual understanding of the matter as appropriate to the developmental stage of the students
C 7.2 – States questions related to matters in the curriculum for which current scientific understanding is well-recognised

competency_groups.title: Explores the nature of Science by doing Science
competency_groups.description: Develops accurate models to represent real-life events and predict results, and designs and implements plans for scientific inquiry including hypothesis formulation, variable identification, data representation, inference drawing, and communication.
competencies: C 8.1 – Develops accurate and appropriate models (including geometric, mathematical, graphical) to represent real-life events and phenomena using scientific principles, and use these models to manipulate variables and predict results

C 8.2 – Designs and implements a plan for scientific inquiry (formulates hypotheses, makes predictions, identifies variables, accurately uses scientific instruments, represents data — primary and secondary — in multiple modes, draws inferences based on data, and understanding of scientific concepts, theories, laws and principles, and communicates findings using scientific terminology)

Investigates the nature and properties of chemical substances (distillation, crystallisation, chromatography, centrifugation, types and properties of mixtures, solutions, colloids, and suspensions).
Describes and represents chemical interactions and changes using symbols and chemical equations (acid and base, metal and non-metal, reversible and irreversible).

outcomes: Explains the role of cellular components (nucleus, mitochondria, endoplasmic reticulum, vacuoles, chloroplast, cell wall), including the semi-permeability of cell membrane in making cell the structural basis of living organisms and functional basis of life processes.

Analyses similarities and differences in the life processes involved in nutrition (photosynthesis in plants; absorption of nutrients in fungi; digestion in animals), transport (transport of water in plants; circulation in animals), exchange of materials (respiration and excretion), and reproduction.
Describes the mechanisms of heredity (in terms of DNA, genes, chromosomes) and variation (as changes in the sequence of DNA).

Analyses evidences of biological evolution demonstrating the consequences of the process of natural selection in terms of changes — in allele frequency in population, structure, and function of organisms.

curriculumSnapshot.overallDescription: 6 units, 6 chapters, 1 topics, 0 subtopics, and 27 learning outcomes extracted through raw extraction, document-structure hierarchy building, node enrichment, normalized teaching blocks, validation, and statistics generation.

units.description: Unit 1: Matter and its Interactions, Unit 2: The Physical World, Unit 3: The Living World, Life Processes and Heredity (Implicit Unit based on CG 3), Interconnectedness between Organisms and Environment (Implicit Unit based on CG 4), Linkages between Scientific Knowledge and Other Curricular Areas (Implicit Unit based on CG 5), Contribution of India to Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 6), Current Discoveries and Frontiers in Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 7), Nature of Science by Doing Science (Implicit Unit based on CG 8), Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour, World of Living, Natural Phenomena, Unit IV: Effects of Current, Unit V: Natural Resources, Unit-II (Additional), Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water

Study of the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water

Finding the pH of the following samples by using pH paper/universal indicator: Dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution.
Studying the properties of acids and bases (HCl & NaOH) on the basis of their reaction with: Litmus solution (Blue/Red), Zinc metal, Solid sodium carbonate.

Performing and observing the following reactions and classifying them into: Combination reaction, Decomposition reaction, Displacement reaction, Double displacement reaction. Action of water on quicklime, Action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, Iron nails kept in copper sulphate solution, Reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions.

Observing the action of Zn, Fe, Cu and Al metals on the following salt solutions: ZnSO4 (aq), FeSO4 (aq), CuSO4 (aq), Al2(SO4)3 (aq). Arranging Zn, Fe, Cu and Al (metals) in the decreasing order of reactivity based on the above result.

Periodic Classification of Elements
Acids, Bases and Salts
Metals and Non-metals
Carbon and its Compounds

Chemical reactions, Chemical equation, Balanced chemical equation, types of chemical reactions: combination, decomposition, displacement, double displacement, precipitation, endothermic exothermic reactions, oxidation and reduction
Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, Modern Periodic Table and the Modern, Metallic and Non-metallic Properties

Acids and Bases – definitions in terms of furnishing of H+ and OH- ions, identification using indicators, chemical properties, examples and uses, neutralization, concept of pH scale (Definition relating to logarithm not required), importance of pH in everyday life; preparation and uses of Sodium Hydroxide, Bleaching Powder, Baking soda, Washing soda and Plaster of Paris
Properties of metals and non-metals; Reactivity series; Formation and properties of ionic compounds; Basic metallurgical processes; Corrosion and its prevention

Covalent bonds – formation and properties of covalent compounds, Versatile nature of carbon, Hydrocarbons – saturated and unsaturated Homologous series. Nomenclature of alkanes, alkenes, alkyne and carbon compounds containing functional groups (halogens, alcohol, ketones, aldehydes). Chemical properties of carbon compounds (combustion, oxidation, addition and substitution reaction). Ethanol and Ethanoic acid (only properties and uses), soaps and detergents
Chemical Reactions
Periodic Classification
Acids Bases Salts
Metals Non-metals
Carbon Compounds
Life processes
Control and co-ordination in animals and plants
'Living Being'. Basic concept of nutrition, respiration, transport and excretion in plants and animals
Tropic movements in plants; Introduction of plant hormones; Control and co-ordination in animals: Nervous system; Voluntary, involuntary and reflex action; Chemical co-ordination: animal hormones
Reproduction in animals and plants (asexual and sexual) reproductive health - need and methods of family planning. Safe sex vs HIV/AIDS. Child bearing and women's health
Heredity; Mendel's contribution- Laws for inheritance of traits: Sex determination; brief introduction
Control and Co-ordination
Reflection of light by curved surfaces
Refraction
Refraction of light by spherical lens
Images formed by spherical mirrors, centre of curvature, principal axis, principal focus, focal length, mirror formula (Derivation not required), magnification
Laws of refraction, refractive index
Image formed by spherical lenses; Lens formula (Derivation not required); Magnification. Power of a lens
Reflection
Spherical Mirrors and Lenses
Functioning of a lens in human eye, defects of vision and their corrections, applications of spherical mirrors and lenses.
Refraction of light through a prism, dispersion of light, scattering of light, applications in daily life (excluding colour of the sun at sunrise and sunset).
Electric current, potential difference and electric current. Ohm's law; Resistance, Resistivity, Factors on which the resistance of a conductor depends.
Series combination of resistors, parallel combination of resistors and its applications in daily life.
Heating effect of electric current and its applications in daily life.
Electric power, Interrelation between P, V, I and R.

Magnetic effects of current: Magnetic field, field lines, field due to a current carrying conductor, field due to current carrying coil or solenoid; Force on current carrying conductor, Fleming's Left Hand Rule, Direct current.
Alternating current: frequency of AC. Advantage of AC over DC. Domestic electric circuits.
Motor
Electromagnetic Induction
Electric Generator
Theme: How Things Work
The following topics are included in the syllabus but will be assessed only formatively to reinforce understanding without adding to summative assessments.
Our environment: Eco-system, Environmental problems, Ozone depletion, waste production and their solutions.
Biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.
Theme: Natural Resources
Identification of the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram or red kidney bean)

topics: Chemical Reactions and Equations

formative_content_refs.title: Practicals: Finding pH, Properties of Acids and Bases, Chemical Reactions, Reactivity Series
Practicals: Dependence of Potential Difference on Current

practicals.title: Finding pH of Various Samples
practicals.details: Find the pH of dilute Hydrochloric Acid, Dilute NaOH solution, Dilute Ethanoic Acid solution, Lemon juice, Water, and Dilute Hydrogen Carbonate solution using pH paper/universal indicator.

practicals.title: Properties of Acids and Bases
practicals.details: Study the properties of acids (HCl) and bases (NaOH) based on their reaction with Litmus solution, Zinc metal, and Solid sodium carbonate.

practicals.title: Classification of Chemical Reactions
practicals.details: Perform and observe reactions including action of water on quicklime, action of heat on ferrous sulphate crystals, iron nails in copper sulphate solution, and reaction between sodium sulphate and barium chloride solutions. Classify them as combination, decomposition, displacement, or double displacement.

practicals.title: Reactivity Series of Metals
practicals.details: Observe the action of Zn, Fe, Cu, and Al metals on salt solutions (ZnSO4, FeSO4, CuSO4, Al2(SO4)3). Arrange the metals in decreasing order of reactivity based on results.

practicals.title: Ohm's Law Verification
practicals.details: Study the dependence of potential difference (V) across a resistor on the current (I) passing through it, determine its resistance, and plot a graph between V and I.

practicals.title: Equivalent Resistance Calculation
practicals.details: Determine the equivalent resistance of two resistors when connected in series and parallel.

practicals.title: Stomata Observation
practicals.details: Prepare a temporary mount of a leaf peel to show stomata.

practicals.title: Respiration and CO2 Release
practicals.details: Experimentally show that carbon dioxide is given out during respiration.

practicals.title: Properties of Acetic Acid
practicals.details: Study the properties of acetic acid (ethanoic acid) including odour, solubility in water, effect on litmus, and reaction with Sodium Hydrogen Carbonate.

practicals.title: Soap Cleaning Capacity Test
practicals.details: Study the comparative cleaning capacity of a sample of soap in soft and hard water.

practicals.title: Focal Length Determination
practicals.details: Determine the focal length of a concave mirror and a convex lens by obtaining the image of a distant object.

practicals.title: Refraction through Glass Slab
practicals.details: Trace the path of a ray of light passing through a rectangular glass slab for different angles of incidence. Measure the angle of incidence, angle of refraction, and angle of emergence.

practicals.title: Binary Fission and Budding Study
practicals.details: Study binary fission in Amoeba and budding in yeast and Hydra with the help of prepared slides.

practicals.title: Light Refraction through Prism
practicals.details: Trace the path of the rays of light through a glass prism.

practicals.title: Embryo Identification
practicals.details: Identify the different parts of an embryo of a dicot seed (pea, gram, or red kidney bean).

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Periodic Classification of Elements
activities.details: Learners read about Döbereiner's Triads, Newlands' Law of Octaves, Mendeléev's Periodic Table, and the Modern Periodic Table. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to reinforce understanding without summative assessment stress.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Heredity and Evolution
activities.details: Learners read about Acquired and Inherited Traits, Speciation, Evolution and Classification, Tracing Evolutionary Relationships, Fossils, and Human Evolution. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio.

activities.title: Portfolio Write-up on Electric Effects of Current
activities.details: Learners read about Motors, Electromagnetic Induction, and Electric Generators. They prepare a brief write-up for their portfolio to gain conceptual clarity.

activities.title: Case Study on Science in Human Life
activities.details: Examine a case study related to the use of Science in human life from the perspective of Social Sciences and Ethics (e.g., Marie Curie, Jenner, treatment of patients with mental illnesses, the story of the atomic bomb, green revolution and GMOs, conservation of biodiversity).

activities.title: Science in Arts and Literature Exploration
activities.details: Explore how literature and arts have influenced Science.

projects.title: Construct Simple Machines
projects.details: Demonstrate the principle of mechanical advantage by constructing simple machines, specifically a system of levers and pulleys.

projects.title: Environmental Problem Solutions Project
projects.details: Investigate environmental problems such as ozone depletion and waste production, and propose solutions for biodegradable and non-biodegradable substances.

marks_distribution: Unit I: Chemical Substances - Nature and Behaviour (25 Marks)
Unit II: World of Living (25 Marks)
Unit IV: Effects of Current (13 Marks)
question_paper_design: Typology of Questions: VSA (including objective type), Assertion-Reasoning, SA, LA, Source-based/Case-based/Passage-based/Integrated assessment questions
bloom_levels: Demonstrate Knowledge and Understanding (State, name, list, identify, define, suggest, describe, outline, summarize)
Application of Knowledge/Concepts (Calculate, illustrate, show, adapt, explain, distinguish)
Formulate, Analyze, Evaluate and Create (Interpret, analyze, compare, contrast, examine, evaluate, discuss, construct)
practical_assessment: List of Experiments (14 items covering Units I, II, III, IV)
Assessment of Practical Skills in Science - Class X (CBSE Publication)
Topics assessed formatively only: Periodic Classification of Elements, Heredity and Evolution, Electric Effects of Electric Current (Portfolio/Periodic Assessment credit)
project_assessment: Portfolio preparation for non-examined topics (Periodic Classification, Heredity & Evolution, Electric Effects)
Joyful and experiential opportunities for internal assessment
